Definition (EN): The SysML metamodel extends the KerML metamodel as specified in the KerML specification [KerML].
Context/Example (EN): The SysML metamodel extends the KerML metamodel as specified in the KerML specification [KerML].

Definition (EN): The KerML metamodel as specified in the KerML specification [KerML].
Context/Example (EN): The SysML metamodel extends the KerML metamodel as specified in the KerML specification [KerML].

Definition (EN): The SysML concrete syntax includes a textual notation (see 8.2.2 ), which is generally distinct from that of KerML, though consistent on common elements (such as packages and expressions), and a complete graphical notation (see 8.2.3 ).
Context/Example (EN): The SysML concrete syntax includes a textual notation (see 8.2.2 ), which is generally distinct from that of KerML, though consistent on common elements (such as packages and expressions), and a complete graphical notation (see 8.2.3 ).

Definition (EN): The SysML concrete syntax includes a textual notation (see 8.2.2 ).
Context/Example (EN): The SysML concrete syntax includes a textual notation (see 8.2.2 ).

Definition (EN): The SysML concrete syntax includes a complete graphical notation (see 8.2.3 ).
Context/Example (EN): ... and a complete graphical notation (see 8.2.3 ).

Definition (EN): The SysML abstract syntax (see 8.3 ) imports the KerML abstract syntax, reusing some KerML metaclasses directly, and further specializing most other KerML metaclasses.
Context/Example (EN): The SysML abstract syntax (see 8.3 ) imports the KerML abstract syntax, reusing some KerML metaclasses directly, and further specializing most other KerML metaclasses.

Definition (EN): The KerML abstract syntax, reusing some KerML metaclasses directly, and further specializing most other KerML metaclasses.
Context/Example (EN): The SysML abstract syntax (see 8.3 ) imports the KerML abstract syntax, reusing some KerML metaclasses directly, and further specializing most other KerML metaclasses.

Definition (EN): The SysML semantics (see 8.4 ) are defined by relating the SysML abstract syntax to the semantic models in the Systems Model Library (see Clause 9 ).
Context/Example (EN): The SysML semantics (see 8.4 ) are defined by relating the SysML abstract syntax to the semantic models in the Systems Model Library (see Clause 9 ).

Equivalent (JP): システムモデルライブラリ
Definition (EN): The semantic models in the Systems Model Library (see Clause 9 ).
Context/Example (EN): The SysML semantics (see 8.4 ) are defined by relating the SysML abstract syntax to the semantic models in the Systems Model Library (see Clause 9 ).

Equivalent (JP): カーネルモデルライブラリ
Definition (EN): The Kernel Model Library from KerML.
Context/Example (EN): ...which is based on the Kernel Model Library from KerML...

Definition (EN): providing syntactic transformations from SysML models to syntactically equivalent KerML models (including elements that are otherwise implicit in the SysML abstract syntax).
Context/Example (EN): ...providing syntactic transformations from SysML models to syntactically equivalent KerML models (including elements that are otherwise implicit in the SysML abstract syntax).

Definition (EN): Names of metaclasses appear exactly as in the abstract syntax, including capitalization, except possibly with added pluralization. When used as English common nouns, e.g., "a Usage", "multiple Subsettings", they refer to instances of the metaclass.
Context/Example (EN): Names of metaclasses appear exactly as in the abstract syntax, including capitalization, except possibly with added pluralization.

Definition (EN): Names of properties of metaclasses, when used as English common nouns, e.g., “an ownedUsage”, “multiple nestedActions”, refer to values of the properties. This can be modified using the term "metaproperty" as necessary to refer to the metaproperty itself instead of its values.
Context/Example (EN): This can be modified using the term "metaproperty" as necessary to refer to the metaproperty itself instead of its values...

Definition (EN): Convention 1 above applies to SysML Definitions (e.g., Action), using "definition" (or a more specialized term) instead of "metaclass" (e.g., "the action definition Action").
Context/Example (EN): Convention 1 above applies to SysML Definitions (e.g., Action), using "definition" (or a more specialized term) instead of "metaclass"...

Definition (EN): Convention 2 above applies to SysML Usages (e.g, actions), using "usage" (or a more specialized term) instead of "metaproperty" (e.g., "the action usage actions").
Context/Example (EN): Convention 2 above applies to SysML Usages (e.g, actions), using "usage" (or a more specialized term) instead of "metaproperty"...

Definition (EN): SysML v2 provides an additional set of Domain Libraries (see 9.4 and following) to provide a set of reference models in various domains important to systems modeling (such as Analysis and Quantities and Units).
Context/Example (EN): Finally, SysML v2 provides an additional set of Domain Libraries (see 9.4 and following)...

Definition (EN): Concrete syntax specifies how the language appears to modelers. Modelers construct and review models using a textual notation that conforms to the concrete syntax specification (see 8.2 ).
Context/Example (EN): Concrete syntax specifies how the language appears to modelers. Modelers construct and review models using a textual notation that conforms to the concrete syntax specification (see 8.2 ).

Definition (EN): Abstract syntax specifies linguistic terms and relations between them (as opposed to library model terms), which may be expressed in the concrete syntax (see 8.3 ).
Context/Example (EN): Abstract syntax specifies linguistic terms and relations between them (as opposed to library model terms), which may be expressed in the concrete syntax (see 8.3 ).

Definition (EN): Semantics specifies the interpretation of models as representations of or specifications for modeled systems (see 8.4 ).
Context/Example (EN): Semantics specifies the interpretation of models as representations of or specifications for modeled systems (see 8.4 ).

Definition (EN): The Root Layer defines the syntactic foundation KerML and, as such, does not have a semantic interpretation relative to the modeled system.
Context/Example (EN): The Root Layer defines the syntactic foundation KerML and, as such, does not have a semantic interpretation relative to the modeled system.

Definition (EN): The Core Layer is grounded in mathematical semantics, supported by the Base package from the Kernel Model Library (see 9.2 ).
Context/Example (EN): The Core Layer is grounded in mathematical semantics, supported by the Base package from the Kernel Model Library (see 9.2 ).

Definition (EN): The Kernel Layer is given semantics fully through its relationship to the Model Library (see Clause 9 ).
Context/Example (EN): The Kernel Layer is given semantics fully through its relationship to the Model Library (see Clause 9 ).
